statement: An OCP system can be formalized as a protected/disturbance/correction tuple with exact and asymptotic branches.
If H = S ⊕ D with S orthogonal to D, orthogonal projection onto S exactly recovers the protected component of any state s+d.
If S ∩ D is nontrivial, no single-valued recovery map can recover s from x=s+d for all s in S and d in D.
The flow xdot = -k P_D x leaves S fixed and exponentially suppresses D.
Knill-Laflamme style exact correction fits OCP when syndrome sectors are orthogonal and recoverable.
Periodic Helmholtz projection gives an exact OCP recovery operator for divergence-free fields under additive gradient contamination.
GLM divergence cleaning fits OCP as an asymptotic correction architecture rather than an exact projector.
Observer/controller designs instantiate OCP when the protected/disturbance split is invariant and feedback acts only on disturbance coordinates.
There exists one scalar correction-capacity number that nontrivially classifies QEC, MHD projection, GLM, and control architectures.
Current optimizer work is a genuine OCP instantiation rather than a loose bridge.
OCP yields practical design rules for building correction operators and recognizing when a correction architecture is underpowered.
OCP gives a meaningful bridge between discrete exact correction and continuous asymptotic correction.
If K annihilates S, preserves D, and its restriction to D is exponentially stable, then the flow xdot=-Kx preserves S and asymptotically suppresses D.
If K is self-adjoint positive semidefinite with ker(K)=S and spectral gap lambda_*>0 on S^perp, then ||e^{-tK}x-P_S x|| <= e^{-lambda_* t}||P_D x||.
If P_S K P_D is nonzero, then disturbances generically leak into the protected coordinates and the flow is not an OCP correction flow preserving S pointwise.
For an exact linear recovery on V = S ⊕ D with correction operator C = I - R, one must have rank(C) >= dim(D) and rank(R) >= dim(S).
Pairwise orthogonal nonzero exact-correction sectors require at least as many distinguishable correction labels as sectors.
OCP supports branch-specific correction-capacity notions even though a universal scalar capacity remains unsupported.
Pairwise orthogonal coordinate-compatible disturbance sectors admit an exact sector-conditioned recovery operator mapping each sector back to the protected space.

claim_name: Finite-time exact recovery no-go for smooth linear flows
statement: If D is nontrivial, a finite-time linear flow e^{-tK} cannot satisfy Φ_t(s+d)=s for all s in S, d in D at any finite t>0.
If two candidate disturbance sectors overlap nontrivially, exact unique sector detection is impossible on the shared family.
On projection-compatible domains, Coulomb-gauge or transverse-field projection is an exact protected-state correction fit by the same projector logic as the Helmholtz/Leray branch.

claim_name: Periodic projector transplant to bounded domains
statement: The periodic exact projector can be reused unchanged as an exact bounded-domain correction operator preserving the bounded protected class.

claim_name: Constraint damping in additional physics systems
statement: Constraint damping architectures in systems such as numerical relativity fit the asymptotic OCP branch when a protected/constraint-violation split and a stable correction generator are made explicit.

Continuous quantum error correction and feedback architectures fit the asymptotic or measurement-conditioned OCP branches when syndrome information remains sufficiently distinguishable and feedback preserves the code sector.
Generic constrained Hamiltonian systems automatically determine a nontrivial OCP correction architecture.
Periodic incompressible velocity projection is an exact protected-state correction fit by the same Helmholtz/Hodge projector logic as the exact continuous branch.
On a nontrivial bounded incompressible protected class, any recovery map that factors only through the divergence scalar field cannot be an exact recovery operator.
Bounded-domain incompressible projection belongs to the exact branch only when the correction step realizes the domain-compatible Hodge projector for the actual protected class and boundary data.
A protected variable is exactly recoverable from a constrained record map if and only if it is constant on the record fibers; equivalently κ_{M,p}(0)=0.
For x = Fz, M(x)=Ox, and p(x)=Lx on a finite-dimensional family, exact linear protected-variable recovery exists if and only if ker(O F) is contained in ker(L F).

A fixed computational-basis record cannot exactly recover a phase-sensitive protected variable on a qubit family that contains the same amplitudes with varying phase, even though weaker protected variables such as the z coordinate remain recoverable.
On the tested periodic incompressible family, full vorticity is an exact protected-variable record, truncated vorticity is approximate only, and divergence-only observation is a no-go.

In the two-state scalar-output control toy model, one-step exact recovery fails, two-step protected-variable recovery is exact whenever ε(a-b) ≠ 0, and observer-based asymptotic recovery still converges from the ongoing record.
For any estimator receiving records with adversarial observation error at most η, the worst-case protected-variable error is bounded below by κ_{M,p}(η)/2.
On a finite-dimensional admissible linear family x=Fz with record OFz and protected variable LFz, exact protected-variable recovery implies rank(OF) >= rank(LF).

For the fixed-basis qubit record on the phase-window family φ ∈ [-w,w], the full Bloch-vector fiber ambiguity satisfies κ(0)=2 sin(min(w, π/2)); exact full recovery holds only at w=0, while weaker protected variables such as z remain exactly recoverable.

claim_name: Periodic cutoff threshold on the two-mode family
statement: For the tested two-mode periodic incompressible family, truncated-vorticity recovery is exact if and only if the Fourier cutoff retains both active modes, which first occurs at cutoff 2.

On the tested four-mode periodic incompressible modal family, exact recovery of a protected linear functional from truncated vorticity is possible if and only if the cutoff retains every Fourier mode used by that functional; the minimal exact cutoff therefore equals the largest protected mode cutoff.

For a nested finite-dimensional linear observation family x=Fz with records O_r F z and protected variable L F z, exact recovery turns on at the first level r for which row(L F) is contained in row(O_r F).

A fixed coarse record can exactly recover a weaker protected variable while exact recovery of a stronger protected variable remains impossible whenever the weaker protected row lies in the observation row space and the stronger one does not.

In the tested scalar-output diagonal family with distinct active eigenvalues, exact recovery of a protected linear functional g·x_0 is possible at horizon H if and only if g_j / c_j agrees on the active sensor spectrum with a polynomial of degree at most H-1, with hidden protected directions remaining impossible; the coordinate threshold is a special case.

On the bounded coefficient family A_B = {F z : ||z||_∞ ≤ B}, the structured collision gap Γ_r(B) is monotone along nested record families, vanishes exactly at the first exact-recovery level, and yields a zero-noise lower bound Γ_r(B)/2 below threshold.

On a bounded-domain family built from boundary-compatible divergence-free stream modes and Dirichlet gradient modes, the orthogonal projector onto the protected span is an exact bounded-domain correction operator.

For a restricted linear family x = F z with record O F z and protected variable L F z, the minimum number of unrestricted added linear measurements needed for exact recovery is δ(O, L; F) = rank([O F; L F]) - rank(O F).
If exact recovery holds on a restricted linear family and K satisfies K O F = L F, then κ_{M,p}(δ) ≤ ||K||_2 δ for Euclidean record and protected metrics.
In the restricted-linear branch, same-rank record families can have opposite exact-recovery verdicts; observation amount alone does not determine exact protected-variable recoverability.

If q = φ ∘ p, exact recoverability of p implies exact recoverability of q, while the converse can fail even on finite or restricted-linear families; exact recovery of such a nonconstant coarsening while p fails is the branch detectable-only regime.

For every n > r ≥ 1 and every r ≤ k < n, there exist restricted finite-dimensional linear recoverability problems with the same ambient dimension n, protected rank r, and observation rank k but opposite exactness verdicts, so no classifier depending only on (n, rank(LF), rank(OF)) can decide exact recoverability on all such families.

claim_name: No fixed-library budget-only exact classifier theorem
statement: For every n > r ≥ 1 and every r ≤ k < n, there exists a common coordinate candidate library with unit measurement costs and two selections of the same size k and same total cost k that have opposite exact-recovery verdicts, so fixed-library budget/count alone cannot decide exact recoverability on all restricted finite-dimensional linear families.

On a restricted linear family x = F z, if a weaker target W F z is exactly recoverable by K O F = W F while a stronger target S F z has positive collision gap Γ on the same bounded family, then bounded record noise ||e|| ≤ η yields a weak-target error upper bound ||K||_2 η while every decoder for the stronger target still has worst-case error at least Γ/2; in particular the weak upper bound stays strictly below the stronger impossibility floor for η < Γ/(2||K||_2).

Let F_s and F_l be restricted-linear admissible family bases with span(F_s) contained in span(F_l). If exact recovery holds on the smaller family but ker(O F_l) is not contained in ker(L F_l), then exact recovery fails on the enlarged family; moreover the enlarged-family collision gap Γ_l yields the lower bound Γ_l/2 for every decoder on the enlarged family, including any decoder exact on the smaller family.

On the canonical restricted-linear family F_beta = span{e1, e2 + beta e3} with record M(x) = (x1, x2) and target p(x) = x3, each fixed beta yields exact target identifiability, but the decoder exact on F_beta0 incurs worst-case exact-data target error |beta - beta0| / sqrt(1 + beta^2) on the true family F_beta over the unit coefficient box. Thus exact identifiability of the true family does not imply robustness of a mismatched inverse map.
